Re-measure at HEAD after the selfSummary() refactor (behaviour identical to bb07648's glyph-gate). Now backed by a unit test (selfSummary.test.ts): host name 'ser581555022561'→null, bare 'Claude Code'→null, '✳/⠐ summary'→summary (glyph stripped), glyph-only→null. So the boot-time host-name and splash ticks never reach the headline; only the agent's glyph-led self-summary does. Deterministic+unit evidence; browser screenshot of a booting row holding its placeholder still owed for a product-level --pass.

CORE HOLDS: live working rows' headlines ARE the workers' tmux self-summaries (完成前端E2E测试和图标系统迁移验证 / 修复键盘交互回归问题), not node/branch names; offline/queued rows show launch-prompt placeholders; op tally (~1) + colour-coded status glyph present. FAIL on the declared two-row face: the map-side SessionWindow renders the COMPACT one-line row (status word collapsed to glyph) since 596cdf10's console unify, and SessionRow's non-compact status-WORD branch has NO caller — but session-activity's spec desc (edited later, 148203d2) still promises 'status + op tally ride a quieter second line on the map-side face'. Spec/code conflict needs reconciliation: either restore the map-side two-line face or rewrite the desc+scenario to the one-line-everywhere design. Not an icon-migration regression (predates it). Boot-flicker sub-case not re-measured (no fresh dispatch this round).

The measured surface is GONE: .si-th-name exists only in styles.css — no JSX renders it. Commit 7092d23a (2026-07-01, CODE-ONLY, no spec/yatsu change: 'drop tab-bar headline… sidebar already identifies the session') removed the action-strip headline, so the console shows NO headline over the terminal and 'strip matches row headline' cannot hold. Silent spec↔code divergence: either restore the strip or retire/rewrite this scenario + the spec clause. Not an icon-migration regression (predates it).

Re-measured on the two NEW harnesses (opencode + pi), both paneTitleIsSelfSummary=false. Real sessions on the worktree dashboard (vite :5176 → spex serve :8909): opencode session ba5610c2's tmux pane title is the bare app name 'OpenCode'; pi session 8d5595e2's is 'π - 阅读-spec-dashboard-src-session-js-总结-sessionHeadli-8d55' (π + worktree FOLDER name). Board rows read via headless Chromium: BOTH headlines are the launch-prompt task preview (阅读 spec-cli/src/harness.ts…/阅读 spec-dashboard/src/session.js…) — never the app name, never the folder slug. The codex-camp fork (activity suppressed → promptPreview) holds for both new adapters.

Re-measured on the NEW harnesses (opencode/pi, paneTitleIsSelfSummary=false) — the semantics fork: here the CORRECT steady headline is the launch-prompt preview, and the anti-flicker claim is what's at stake. Filmed a fresh pi session (b8d70266) booting on the live board (worktree dashboard :5176 → serve :8909), sampling the row's headline every 1s for 75s: row appears queued(◌) with the prompt preview, flips working(●) at ~3s, and the headline NEVER changes — while tmux's actual pane title was 'π - BOOTWATCH检查-harness-分层与-liveness-判定-只读浏览-spec-cli-b8d7' (π + worktree folder slug) the whole time. No host-name flash, no app-name splash, no folder slug ever surfaces; one compact line, glyph+tally inline, avatar kept on the map-side window. opencode steady-state corroborated on session ba5610c2 (pane title 'OpenCode', headline = task preview).

Re-measured with the NEW harnesses live (opencode ba5610c2 asking + pi 8d5595e2/b8d70266 working; paneTitleIsSelfSummary=false → headline = prompt preview). Real browser on #/sessions (worktree vite :5176 → serve :8909): .si-th-name absent from the DOM, the strip over the terminal carries ONLY the tabs (terminal|eval); the console names the session once in the sidebar — the selected pi row's headline is verbatim the board-window row's line (all 14 sidebar rows == board rows, zero mismatches), and it un-truncates in place when selected (row wraps to 64.7px, neighbours stay single-line). No stable node/branch handle appears as a console title anywhere.

Re-measured with NEW-harness sessions live (opencode/pi). Display halves PASS: palette SESSION rows title with the exact board headline (pi '阅读 spec-dashboard/src/session.js,总结…', opencode '阅读 spec-cli/src/harness.ts,总结各…'), and the lock-hint banner reads the same headline verbatim. But the stable-handle matching claim FAILS — and not only for the new harnesses: palette queries by session-id fragment return ZERO session rows for '8d55', '8d5595e2', 'ba5610', AND '9282bab6' (a claude session). Root cause read off the live code: SpecSearch puts `${promptPreview} ${sessionHandle(s)}` in the match body, but sessionHandle = s.label || s.name || s.node || s.title || s.branch || s.id (session.js:65) short-circuits on the server-derived `label` — always present since the session-label wire work — so node/branch/id NEVER enter the match body. 'BOOTWATCH' still matching the renamed session shows only promptPreview riding. The comment above sessionHandle ('search still matches node/branch/id') directly contradicts the behaviour. Filing issue.

Real browser #/sessions on the worktree dashboard: at rest every sidebar row is one line with ellipsis; HOVER on a truncated row leaves geometry untouched (height 24px → 24px, background tint only — no expand, list stays a stable click surface); CLICK selects it and the headline un-truncates in place (row 24→37px, sess-id wraps to full text, scrollWidth no longer exceeds clientWidth) while every neighbour stays single-line (25–27px); the markers (status glyph) stay pinned to the first line's top-right (sess-meta rect at the row's top-right corner), wrapped text running full width beneath. Harness-irrelevant by design (pure styles.css) — measured against the same tree as the B-batch.
